expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md, section “1) Case focus is explicitly covered…”, the run asserts Phase 4b purpose: “Group the Phase 4a draft into the canonical top-layer taxonomy without merging away scenario granularity.” It also lists “Anti-Compression Rule” guardrails such as “Do not merge distinct Workstation-only and Library-gap scenarios…”. This directly matches the expectation focus.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: In result.md, section “2) Output aligns with the primary phase under test: phase4b”, the run states Phase 4b work/output: “spawn the canonical top-layer grouper”, output “phase4b_spawn_manifest.json”, and required output “drafts/qa_plan_phase4b_r<round>.md”, and notes phase constraints like “Do not apply few-shot cleanup in this phase; Phase 6 owns…”. This indicates the evaluated output is framed as Phase 4b contract/workflow alignment.
